thinking: I face a sparse cafe/break area with a yellow rectangular table and chairs on the left, a seated female customer at the far side of the table, and a kitchenette with cabinets, sink, kettle, pots, and a refrigerator behind her. I slowly move leftward and closer to the table, circling toward the customer while keeping her in view; the customer remains seated, looking down and then toward me. Midway through, she raises a hand to her face/head as if resting her cheek or signaling mild attention, then lowers it while staying seated. By the end, I approach the near edge of the table and extend my hands toward the tabletop, as if preparing to address her or place/take something.
caption: CLERK: Intent was to approach and attend to the seated customer; I walked closer around the table and reached my hands toward the tabletop to begin service or interaction. CUSTOMER: Intent appeared to be waiting for assistance or acknowledgment; she stayed seated at the table, looked toward me, and briefly touched/raised her hand to her face while watching my approach.
